title: Reflected XSS
description:
A search results page reflects the user's query parameter directly into the HTML response without escaping. An attacker can craft a URL containing a `<script>` tag that executes in the victim's browser when they visit the link.

Fix the `search_page()` function to HTML-escape the query before rendering it.
hint: Use `html.escape(query)` from Python's standard library before inserting the value into the HTML string.
explanation: Reflected XSS occurs when untrusted data is included in an HTTP response without proper encoding. HTML-escaping converts `<`, `>`, `"`, and `&` to safe entities so the browser renders them as text rather than interpreting them as HTML tags.
canonical_fix:
import html def search_page(query): safe_query = html.escape(query) return f'

Results for: {safe_query}

'

attack_flow.label: Browser sees
attack_flow.value: <script> tag treated as HTML — JavaScript executes immediately

attack_flow.label: Result
attack_flow.value: Attacker JS runs in victim's browser; can steal cookies or redirect

why_blocked: `html.escape()` converts `<` to `<` and `>` to `>`, so `<script>alert(1)</script>` becomes the harmless text `<script>alert(1)</script>`. The browser renders it as visible text instead of executing it as code.
